source.title: How to attack Chalet top floor via Solar/Master (not the Library/Mezzanine/Bar side)
source.notes: Single-site walkthrough for the Solar/Master site (same site as chalet_defense.json's 'salarium-master'), deliberately attacking from the normally-ignored east/Solar side of the map instead of Library/Mezzanine/Bar. Auto-generated captions: 'Habana'/'a banner' = Hibana (hard breacher, interchangeable with Ace); 'doabe'/'do could be' = Dokkaebi (confirmed by context — Logic Bomb 'calls', SMG-11 loadout, holding a flank window; ASR mangled her name throughout, corrected here); 'cap'/'capal'/'capow'/'Capital' = Capitao (fire turret area denial); 'buck'/'bucking' = the operator 'Book' (Blitz is NOT meant — this is the creator's in-game handle/operator choice for the roaming hard-breach-adjacent player who shotguns/mouseholes floors; confirmed by context of shooting through floors and ceilings with a shotgun, i.e. Buck). Where the transcript says 'Book' below it refers to the operator Buck. 'gridlock'/'noad'/'Nomad' both named as interchangeable picks for the Airjab/rotate-hold role. Ground-floor/other-site rooms (Library, Mezzanine, Bar, Gaming, Fireplace) are explicitly stated by the creator as not attacked in this strat and are omitted. Compass directions could not be reliably grounded against a real top-down blueprint during the rewrite pass (no browser/image access at rewrite time) — locations below are room/feature names only, with no compass prefixes, per the spec's rule against inventing directions.

hatches.note: Buck can shoot out this hatch to open a hole through to the top floor. Lets the defending team drop through it too — optional, not required if not playing Buck.

gadgetPlacements.location: Solar Stairs Wall from Solar Repel Windows
gadgetPlacements.note: If a defender holds an unshootable angle on the stairs, open the windows and use Capitao's fire turret or Grim's canister to flush them down into Buck, who is clearing below. Grim doesn't guarantee movement — a defender can sit in the gas/bees.

gadgetPlacements.operator: Capitao (or Ace, using his own charges)
gadgetPlacements.note: One Claymore on the pipe store, a second around the corner of the soft wall (not side-on, so an impact grenade destroying the wall doesn't also destroy the claymore) to punish attackers flanking through an impact hole.
Primary hard breach into Master/Solar corner. Breach at range. Wait until Buck has cleared the room behind the wall or the charge gets shot off.

gadgetPlacements.location: Big Garage Entry, Through Trophy Floor into Solar
gadgetPlacements.note: Comes in through Big Garage, drones down to Trench, then fires shotgun rounds up through the floor in line with the van/Trophy Room marker to blow holes through Trophy floor into Solar — denies anyone standing on that spot and lets the repel players push in. Only do this once the repel players are ready to push.
From the back corner of the basement hatch, shoot up at an angle to destroy the default Solar camera through the ceiling. Non-Buck players can walk up and destroy the same camera manually instead.
Prep-phase drone: climb the white bucket/ladder near Big Garage and place a drone to check if Big Garage is clear and watch whether the Trophy hatch gets opened.
Prep-phase drone: up Solar Stairs, onto the planter, then onto the wooden beam in the rafters. Hard for defenders to spot from below.
Action-phase drone: positioned on a plant then a beam near Ivy window to watch for anyone peeking or playing Highway outside.
Airjab Launcher off both doors to stop defenders flanking the repel players. Cover whichever door isn't hit with a drone instead.

Takes up the rotate position defenders would normally hold. Places an Airjab above the Master door into Piano only once Master is confirmed clear — placing it early gets it shot off. Holds the cutoff into Piano and Bathroom for the round.

Holds a West Main Stairs window (the one with an angle onto the double door is preferred) to catch defenders rotating from Library/Mezzanine through Kitchen/West Main/Trophy Stairs. Shoot only the bottom of the window out to stay inconspicuous; crouch to avoid a head-glitch giveaway.
Post-plant: repel up to Highway and hold the Bathroom window, covered by the earlier Ivy-window drone.
Repels in and plants in the corner to the right of the closet door once the wall is open. Plays passively post-breach — defenders can't jump out through these windows to flank.

operatorRecommendations.note: Breaches the Master/Solar wall at range, repels in, and plants in the corner by the closet door. Wait for the room to be cleared by Buck first. Ace can carry his own Claymores if Hibana is picked.

operatorRecommendations.role: Roam clear / mouseholes (called 'Book' in the transcript)
operatorRecommendations.note: Enters via Big Garage, drones through, then shotguns holes through the Trophy floor into Solar to deny that anchor spot; can also destroy the default Solar camera through the hatch/ceiling. After the plant, rotates up West Main Stairs to help hold or push.
Airjabs the Front Door and Trench Door, then holds the rotate into Piano/Bathroom for most of the round.
Holds a West Main Stairs window pre-plant to punish rotating defenders, then repositions to Highway/Bathroom window post-plant.
Fire turret or canister to flush an unshootable Solar Stairs angle down into Buck; also places Claymores around the pipe store and soft-wall corner.

generalTips.note: This strat ignores the south side of the map (Library, Mezzanine, Bar, Gaming, Fireplace) — defenders place most setup utility there, leaving the Solar/east side comparatively bare (maybe a Frost mat, Goyo, or Melusi at most).
Coordinate Buck (clearing below) and Capitao/Grim (flushing Solar Stairs) — don't flame the stairs before Buck is ready below or the flushed defender runs away safely.
The West Main Stairs window hold is timed around the ~60-second window when defenders realize the push is on Solar and rotate across via Kitchen/West Main/Trophy Stairs.

Minimum 3 players: hard breacher (self-supplies Claymores if playing Ace), area-clear operator, and Buck. West Main Stairs window player is useful but not essential with only 3. Easier with 4, easiest with 5.

Post-plant, as long as Nomad/Gridlock stays alive holding the Piano/Bathroom rotate, defenders cannot approach from that side — their only routes in are up the stairs (denied by Buck), through the Master door (airjabbed/held), or West Main/Highway (held by Dokkaebi/Lion).
Considered an easier attack than the standard south-side push since defenders don't expect it and lose their Solar anchor position quickly once Buck starts damaging the floor beneath it.
